summary: This is the fourth and final installment in a series about building a custom slate-and-wood coffee table. The post is essentially a photo showcase of the completed project, presenting corner detail and isometric views of the finished piece. Shapiro invites readers who are inspired to try similar projects to share their results, and offers to provide more detailed instructions to anyone attempting the build. It's a brief, celebratory wrap-up to a multi-part DIY furniture project.
key_points: The series exceeded its planned 3-part scope, humorously acknowledged in the title
The final post focuses on showcasing the completed coffee table through photographs
Shapiro offers to help others replicate the project with additional instructions on request
money_quotes: Part IV in a 3-or-so part post describing how I made our coffee table.
And finally, the best part of all… the pictures of the final product.
If you're inspired by this project to try something similar, drop me a line and show me your results!
key_insight: A hands-on DIY furniture project wraps up with pride in the finished product and an invitation for others to try building their own.
